Reviews Contact
Home / Services / Fireplace Refacing

Fireplace Refacing

Transforming outdated hearths into Center City showpieces with real masonry.

Philadelphia's 1970s ranch homes and 1990s loft conversions often suffer from builder-grade fireplace surrounds that detract from their potential. We rebuild fireboxes and create custom surrounds using materials ranging from salvaged factory brick to hand-carved limestone.

Every project starts with a safety inspection - we frequently discover improper clearances in flipped properties. For gas inserts popular in Northern Liberties condos, we install proper ventilation and non-combustible surrounds. Wood-burning applications in Mount Airy stone colonials receive soapstone hearths that retain heat safely.

Design options include: arched openings to match original row house proportions, recessed log storage nooks for tight spaces, and integrated display ledges for art collections. All work complies with Philadelphia's strict fireplace ordinances and includes carbon monoxide detector placement guidance.

Our Process

  1. 01 Step 1: Safety inspection and measurements
  2. 02 Step 2: Removal of non-compliant materials
  3. 03 Step 3: Firebox reconstruction as needed
  4. 04 Step 4: Custom surround construction and finishing

Benefits

  • Increases home value more than superficial updates
  • Eliminates draft issues from poorly sized flues
  • Materials selected for easy Philly-friendly maintenance
  • Includes annual inspection discount

Frequently Asked Questions

Can you work around my existing mantel?

We assess structural integrity first. Many vintage mantels need reinforcement before surrounding with new masonry - we preserve original details whenever possible.

What's the most durable material for active families?

How disruptive is the demo phase?
